channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft just got its hands on 100,000 Nvidia GB300 chips - and all it took was investing $33 billion in these startups
item.description: Microsoft’s $33 billion investment in neocloud providers secures Nvidia GPUs, expands compute access, and raises new antitrust and energy concerns.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: This PC is truly unique - Minisforum M1-1295 is the first mini PC with no front-facing ports, and the audiophile in me will more than appreciate the three separate audio connectors
item.description: Minisforum M1-1295 combines minimalist design and Intel power, hiding all ports at the back for a cleaner workstation aesthetic.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan invite families to join their push against predatory social media practices
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Prince Harry and Meghan urged parents to stand against social media companies that they said lure children with predatory algorithms designed to exploit their data amid the “explosion of unregulated artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Intel details new efficient Xeon processor line
item.description: Intel has revealed more details of its next-generation Xeon 6+ E-core CPU family, codenamed “Clearwater Forest,” with a focus on power and performance efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: UK watchdog targets Google's 'strategic' role in search ads and a competitive market
item.description: Britain’s antitrust watchdog is labeling Google a “strategic” player in the online search advertising market, paving the way for regulators to force the company to change its business practices to ensure more competition in that market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Good news forgetful typers - Microsoft Word will now save new documents to OneDrive by default
item.description: Microsoft Word will automatically start sharing docs to OneDrive to ensure users can share more easily.
item.dc:creator: Craig Hale, TechRadar

item.title: TikTok athletes are freaking out about the Strava v. Garmin lawsuit
item.description: For many high-impact runners, it feels like Mom and Dad are fighting. Strava, the popular fitness-tracking app, is suing the fitness wearable giant Garmin over alleged patent infringement and breach of contract.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Too many junk calls to your iPhone? Try Apple’s new iOS 26 call screen feature
item.description: iPhone users have a new tool to combat the scourge of nuisance phone calls: a virtual gatekeeper that can screen incoming calls from unknown numbers. It’s among the bevy of new features that Apple rolled out with last month’s release of iOS 26.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: New York City sues social media companies over youth mental health impacts
item.description: New York City on Wednesday sued several major social media platforms, including Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and YouTube, accusing them of fueling a youth mental health crisis.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: House’s zero trust journey is more process than technology update
item.description: Jamie Crotts, the CIO for the House of Representatives, said improving processes and data governance are driving zero trust principles into their network. The post House’s zero trust journey is more process than technology update first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Princess of Wales suggests parents should put down the phone to combat social discontent
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales has a suggestion for parents: Please put down the phone. Kate, as she is commonly known, collaborated with adult development researcher Robert Waldinger to warn that technology is contributing to an epidemic of disconnection that is hurting family relationships.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Starburst pushes lakehouse boundaries with multi-agent AI and unified vector search
item.description: Starburst is updating its AI services to help enterprises build multi-agent workflows and monitor these agents for compliance and cost effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Infoblox’s Chris Usserman on the benefits of protective DNS
item.description: Recent NIST guidance brings attention to the Domain Name System, an overlooked but critical layer of network security. The Infoblox cyber expert explains why. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Infoblox’s Chris Usserman on the benefits of protective DNS first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Cisco’s Michael Overstreet on securing IT-OT interfaces in federal networks
item.description: Agencies face rising cybersecurity risks as IT and OT converge. Cisco security solutions engineer shares strategies for visibility, segmentation and AI.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gerton, Federal News Network

item.title: From pilot to production: How large enterprises can scale XR projects
item.description: Broad adoption of augmented and virtual reality (AR and VR) technologies remains at an early stage, but many businesses are already seeing value in the systems they’ve rolled out: immersive training for frontline employees, for example, or engineers who can collaborate on design using 3D models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tesla offers cheaper versions of 2 electric vehicles in bid to win back market share in tough year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tesla rolled out new, cheaper versions of two of its electric car models on Tuesday in hopes the offerings will help revive flagging sales but investors dumped its stock anyway.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: 3 big takeaways from The Hill’s Health Next Summit
item.description: The state of the country’s health care landscape is in flux, according to top health experts and lawmakers, as the federal government shutdown hinges on the battle over health care access and new priorities crystallize under the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Kentucky lawsuit says Roblox fails to protect children on its popular online gaming platform
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Kentucky's attorney general claimed Tuesday that the online gaming platform Roblox has become a “playground for predators” as he announced a lawsuit accusing the company of lax child safety measures.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Growing number of people spending less time on social media
item.description: Around 50% of all teens and young adults surveyed said they have taken a break from social media in the past year.
item.dc:creator: Marcus Espinoza, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI and chipmaker AMD sign chip supply partnership for AI infrastructure
item.description: Semiconductor maker AMD will supply its chips to artificial intelligence company OpenAI as part of an agreement to team up on building AI infrastructure, the companies said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5
